hypothesis: For industrial-scale production (≥10 tonnes/year) of Ca2SiO4, augmenting the workflow's economic metric with modeled heating energy consumption (derived from reactant/product heat capacities and reaction enthalpies) and ML-predicted purification costs (based on solubility/volatility estimates) will reverse the cost-optimal recipe from CaO+SiO2 to Ca3SiO5+Si3N4 in at least 70% of realistic market-price and supply-risk scenarios over a 5-year forecast horizon.
